name: Franz Machado
title: Physics PhD Student
bio: I'm a physicist working on neutrino physics and nuclear nonproliferation at Illinois Institute of Technology. My current research focuses on enhancing sub-GeV dark matter detection by implementing the PROSPECT-II antineutrino detector at the Spallation Neutron Source (SNS), and on developing calibration methods for the Mobile Antineutrino Demonstrator (MAD) project.

Previously, I worked at CERN on the LHCb data acquisition system, conducted dark matter detector research at the University of Alberta, and contributed to cosmic-ray detector calibration at Universidad Nacional de Ingeniería in Peru. I am also a member of Los caminos de Feynman, a scientific communication group that promotes the work of Latin-American physicists.
